summary: Init stage complete. Target identified as OWASP Juice Shop (Node.js/Express) at http://127.0.0.1:3010. RoE understood: app_assessment, scope limited to 127.0.0.1/localhost, no off-box post-ex. Initial attack surface map documented from known Juice Shop architecture. Ready for recon stage handoff.

candidates.claim: SQL injection in login form (email/password fields) - classic Juice Shop vulnerability
SQL injection in product search parameter
SQL injection in basket item operations
Login brute force / rate limiting test
Server-Side Template Injection in B2B order API
Path traversal in static file serving
SSRF via product review image URL
Cross-Site Script Inclusion - JSON responses may be contamitable via script tags
Sensitive information disclosure in client-side source

facts.summary: Target is OWASP Juice Shop - a Node.js/Express intentionally vulnerable web application
Scope limited to 127.0.0.1 and localhost only
RoE: app_assessment - no post-ex host takeover off-box, no attacking other hosts/ports
Juice Shop uses SQLite database by default (can be MariaDB)
Juice Shop uses Angular for frontend SPA with REST API backend
Juice Shop uses JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for authentication
Common vulnerability classes: SQLi, XSS, NoSQLi, SSTI, IDOR, Path Traversal, File Upload, CSRF, SSRF, Mass Assignment, GraphQL issues
Juice Shop has a known B2B API endpoint at /b2b/v2/orders vulnerable to SSTI
Juice Shop has an FTP file access surface at /ftp with path traversal protection that can be bypassed
Stage init complete - no live reconnaissance performed yet

raw.summary: Init stage complete. Target identified as OWASP Juice Shop (Node.js/Express) at http://127.0.0.1:3010. RoE understood: app_assessment, scope limited to 127.0.0.1/localhost, no off-box post-ex. Initial attack surface map documented from known Juice Shop architecture. Ready for recon stage handoff.

surfaces.description: REST API endpoints - Juice Shop exposes /api/Users, /api/Products, /api/Basket, /api/Orders, etc.
B2B API endpoint - known SSTI vulnerability surface in Juice Shop
Product search endpoint - potential injection surface
Static file serving - potential path traversal / information disclosure
GraphQL endpoint (present in modern Juice Shop)
FTP file access (path traversal via URL encoding)

candidates.description: SQL injection in login form (email/password fields) - classic Juice Shop vulnerability
candidates.hypothesis: POST /api/Users/login with SQL injection payload in email field may bypass authentication

candidates.description: SQL injection in product search parameter
candidates.hypothesis: GET /api/Products/search?q= injection test

candidates.description: SQL injection in basket item operations
candidates.hypothesis: Basket item endpoints may be vulnerable to SQL injection via product ID or quantity
JSON body with $ne or $gt operators may bypass validation
Search query parameter may be reflected unsanitized in response
Feedback comment may be stored and rendered without sanitization
Angular SPA may process hash fragment unsafely
Authenticated user may access other users' profile data by modifying user ID
User may access/modify another user's basket by changing basket ID
User may access another user's order details
JWT may use weak secret, allow none algorithm, or contain sensitive data

candidates.description: Login brute force / rate limiting test
candidates.hypothesis: Login endpoint may lack rate limiting allowing credential brute force
Password reset token may be predictable or bypassable

candidates.description: Server-Side Template Injection in B2B order API
candidates.hypothesis: B2B order quantity/price template may evaluate user input

candidates.description: Path traversal in static file serving
candidates.hypothesis: Static file endpoints may allow directory traversal with ../
FTP endpoint may allow directory traversal with URL encoding tricks
Profile picture upload may accept .js/.html files leading to XSS or code execution
API may lack CSRF tokens allowing cross-site requests

candidates.description: SSRF via product review image URL
candidates.hypothesis: Image URL parameter may be fetched server-side allowing SSRF

candidates.description: Cross-Site Script Inclusion - JSON responses may be contamitable via script tags
candidates.hypothesis: API JSON responses may be parseable via script tags leading to data exfiltration
Registration may allow setting admin/role fields via JSON body
GraphQL endpoint may expose full schema via introspection query
API may allow arbitrary origins via CORS headers

candidates.description: Sensitive information disclosure in client-side source
candidates.hypothesis: Source maps, API keys, or internal paths may be exposed in JS files
API may accept XML content type and be vulnerable to XXE
